summary: Anne Wojcicki discusses the origins of 23andMe, driven by a desire to shift the healthcare focus toward prevention and empower everyday people. She reflects on the importance of her upbringing, the hard lessons learned from regulatory setbacks with the FDA, and her belief that health is an interplay of genetics and environment.

principles.statement: The healthcare system's financial incentives are misaligned with patient care and prevention.
principles.rationale: Healthcare professionals do not make money by preaching prevention, which leaves the most vulnerable patients without the care they need.
principles.source_quote: I started to see how financial incentives encouraged the healthcare system to not really care for the patients that needed their help the most

principles.statement: Health and life expectancy are a combination of genetics and environment.
principles.rationale: While genetic tests can identify risk factors, how a person lives and their environment play an equally major role in their overall health.
principles.source_quote: Our life expectancy is this beautiful interplay of what we're born with, and how we live. Our environment plays a major role in our health.

principles.statement: Good leadership requires knowing your employees as people, not just as workers.
principles.rationale: Being a hands-on boss means understanding who your team members are personally to foster a better working environment.
principles.source_quote: I like knowing not just about the work someone is doing, but about who they are.

mental_models.name: Healthcare as a Misaligned Incentive Structure
mental_models.description: Viewing the traditional healthcare system through the lens of financial incentives, where the lack of profit in prevention leads to systemic neglect of early care and vulnerable populations.
mental_models.example: Creating 23andMe to bypass the traditional insurance-based system and give everyday people direct access to preventative health information.

heuristics: Don't be afraid if someone disagrees with you.

signature_quotes.text: I started to see how financial incentives encouraged the healthcare system to not really care for the patients that needed their help the most
signature_quotes.context: Explaining her motivation for founding 23andMe after working in healthcare research.

signature_quotes.text: Our life expectancy is this beautiful interplay of what we're born with, and how we live. Our environment plays a major role in our health.
signature_quotes.context: Discussing the limitations and realities of what genetic testing can actually tell a person.

signature_quotes.text: I like knowing not just about the work someone is doing, but about who they are.
signature_quotes.context: Describing her hands-on management style with her 500 employees.

anti_patterns.description: Focusing solely on the long-term vision of the company while neglecting regulatory compliance and validation.
anti_patterns.why_it_fails: It can lead to major regulatory setbacks (like 23andMe's 2013 FDA ban) because you fail to prove clinical and analytical validity to the authorities.
